analysis: An exact algebraic verification proved that no 1-cycles (circuit cycles) exist for m <= 100000 odd steps, other than the trivial m=1, n=1 cycle. The algebraic requirement n = (3^m - 2^m) / (2^k - 3^m) was checked comprehensively using arbitrary-precision arithmetic. This represents a rigorous algebraic theorem matching the Steiner cycle exclusion framework.
